summary: Scorching second set with monster Eyes of the World & Terrapin jam
blurb: The July 8 1990 Three Rivers show builds from a solid first set into a fire‑hot second set. Highlights include Samson & Delilah launching into an epic Eyes Of The World, Let It Grow and a seamless Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → I Need A Miracle sequence. Brent’s keyboards shine and the dual drummers drive the jam to peak energy.
review: On July 8, 1990 the Dead took the massive Three Rivers Stadium crowd on a high‑octane ride that still feels fresh today. After a respectable first set anchored by Touch Of Grey and Let It Grow, the band ignited the second set with a rousing Samson & Delilah, Jerry dancing and Brent blazing on keys. That momentum exploded into an expansive Eyes Of The World that never let up, followed by a soaring Estimated Prophet and a Terrapin Station‑driven marathon that wove through Jam, Drums, Space and landed on a triumphant I Need A Miracle. The set closed with tight renditions of Wang Dang Doodle, Black Peter and Turn On Your Lovelight before the encore’s heartfelt Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door. Jerry’s tone was crisp and his slide on Teach Your Children (heard in other recordings) earned high praise; Phil anchored the groove especially on Tom Thumb Blues, while Bob added rhythm‑guitar fire and even rushed the front of stage during Estimated Prophet. Brent delivered what many called his “last great show,” and Mickey and Bill supplied some of the era’s finest dual‑drum work, driving the jam to a truly scorching climax.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings describe the performance as positive, highlighting a fire‑hot second set, standout jams and strong individual playing; two mixed reviews focus on recording flaws rather than musical quality. Consistent praise across multiple sources for Eyes Of The World, Terrapin jam and Brent’s keyboards supports a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture from the View From The Vault video; clear, well‑balanced mix with no audio flaws and it showcases the scorching second set—including the Terrapin > Drums > Space > Miracle sequence—in top quality.

key_highlights: Monster Eyes Of The World jam that builds into a soaring Let It Grow
Seamless Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → I Need A Miracle sequence
Brent’s fiery keyboard work on Samson & Delilah and throughout the set
Dual‑drum power driving the second‑set jams, cited as some of the era’s best

band_performance.Jerry: Energetic and inspired, delivering crisp tone, soaring solos and a memorable slide on Teach Your Children; he danced during Samson & Delilah and kept the band “traversing the planet.”
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with above‑average work on Tom Thumb’s Blues; his contribution underpinned the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Bob: Fun and rhythmic, adding vocal flair on Tom Thumb lyrics, strong rhythm guitar throughout, and a front‑stage rush during Estimated Prophet that heightened crowd excitement.
band_performance.Brent: On fire for Samson & Delilah and consistently great all night; fans note this as one of his last stellar performances, providing powerful support and melodic depth.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivered top‑tier drumming, part of the era’s best dual‑drum work, especially during extended jams in the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey supplied driving, tight rhythms that propelled the Terrapin jam and kept the momentum blazing throughout the show.
